body.title: sprint-ticket: call advisor on re-dispatch and after 3rd test failure
body.result: Step 2.5 re-dispatch advisor check + Step 8 3rd-failure advisor call added to SKILL.md; levers doc updated
body.intention: I intend that CC reaches for advisor before burning more turns on a stalled sprint — after a prior failure (reset_count > 0) and after 3 consecutive test failures — so human-level diagnosis arrives before the problem compounds.
body.description:
The sprint-ticket skill has no escalation path when CC is spinning. Two insertion points:
1. Step 2 status check: when ticket reset_count > 0, call advisor() before reading files or forming a plan. Brief: 'Ticket T-xxx has been reset N time(s) — prior sprint stalled without closing. What is the most likely root cause and how should I approach this differently?'
2. Step 8 test: when the same tests fail on a 3rd consecutive fix attempt within the session, call advisor() before trying again. Brief: 'Three fix cycles failed on T-xxx. Failing tests: <list>. Attempts so far: <diff summary>. What am I missing?'

**Affected files:** ~/.claude/skills/sprint-ticket/SKILL.md
**Design rules:** none — skill file, not source code
**Scope boundary:** Two advisor insertion points only. No other changes to the sprint-ticket flow. The 3-attempt count is a model guideline, not a hard counter.
**Completion criteria:** SKILL.md contains (a) a Step 2.5 block that reads reset_count from cc_queue.py show and calls advisor() when > 0; (b) a rule in Step 8 directing advisor() call after 3 consecutive test failures.
